generation_status.note: Character/sprite generation for this version is closed. v0.2.0 is a manifest-wiring bump (SPEC-300 §6 C4 / SPEC-301 C4): it adds the corner-Wang terrain tileset declaration (the orc-camp-terrain-wang-topdown.png sheet was already generated) plus the scene.backdrop/decor/shadow consumption contract (SPEC-300 §2.5). No new character assets were generated. Further improvements happen via asset pack version bumps. Deferred enhancements are tracked in docs/13-PixelLab-Asset-Registry.md (Deferred to Future Versions). v0.2.0 also adds the SPEC-304 character avatar `portraits` block: 20 BG-style 2:3 busts (5 base + 15 prestige tiers), owner-provided external pixel art downscaled to 512x512 lossless WebP (dark background baked — framed by web CSS, not transparent; the earlier PixelLab create_map_object probe set was superseded). Per-file SHA-256 + tier(suffix) mapping are tracked in docs/13-PixelLab-Asset-Registry.md (item 12). Runtime consumes a character's tier portrait via the prestige seam (web/src/assets/prestige.ts → displayedTierForOrc; SPEC-302 usage→tier is forward).

terminated.runtime_behavior: static fallback plus status effect only
terminated.note: PixelLab falling-back-death is deprecated for this product and must not be used.

prestige.note: Design SSOT docs/assets/15-Character-State-Model.md §2.2; runtime resolution SSOT docs/specs/SPEC-302-mascot-prestige-tiers.md. thresholds = manifest copy of SPEC-302 §3.1. Phased rollout (SPEC-302 §3.6): T1=available (introduced first; 8-frame v3 animations in progress 2026-06-29), T2/T3=staged (rotations generated + owner-IP-approved but rollout held — excluded from resolution, downward-fallback to highest available tier; flip to available progressively as their animations land). All 3 rotation sets exist; runtime uses static_tier (SPEC-302 §3.4) for an available tier until its animations are wired, and falls back to base (tier 0) for any non-available tier (SPEC-302 §3.3).

orc-claude-storm-shaman.role: Claude agent session character, storm shaman strategist

terminated.runtime_behavior: static fallback plus status effect only

prestige.note: Prestige tiers for orc-claude-storm-shaman. SSOT docs/assets/15-Character-State-Model.md + docs/specs/SPEC-302-mascot-prestige-tiers.md. Phase 1 (2026-06-30): T1=available (8-dir rotation + idle/active/roaming v3 animations; waiting/stale/error -> static_tier per SPEC-302 §3.4). T2/T3=staged = Phase 2 (T2/T3 animations + T3 extreme active + blocking IP review; deferred on generation budget; SPEC-302 §3.6). thresholds = copy of SPEC-302 §3.1.

warbase-sunset-dashboard.usage: legacy camp detail background; superseded as default by orccamp-default

orccamp-default.usage: default camp background (image-ground mode): WORLD = native 3344x1882 (no renderer upscale, world_scale=1) so it is crisp. Built by merging 4x 1672x941 tiles (2x2, feathered overlap) into ~3004x1652 then fitting to 3344x1882. Fixed scale (no zoom), drag-pan only. Place orc sprites inside ground.polygon (cracked-dirt courtyard), clamp targets to the inscribed safe_area. ground.ratio is scale-invariant (0.2815).

gate.accepted: user-approved 2026-06-29 — gothic courtyard is genuinely smaller; kept as a documented per-background exception (SPEC-301 §2.8f)

necropolis-camp.usage: alternate camp background (image-ground): gothic undead necropolis night. WORLD = native 3344x1882, world_scale=1. Cobblestone courtyard with central skull sigil is the walkable ground (measured by scene-placement-engineer). ground.ratio 0.2191 is below the gate (0.2815) but kept as a user-approved documented exception (gate.exception=true, SPEC-301 §2.8f): the gothic courtyard is genuinely smaller. Functional: orcs clamp to safe_area (1360x680, ~18 orc capacity).

froststeel-camp.source_reference: merged from 4x 1672x941 tiles (frost-bg-tile-{a,b,c,d}-3) — feathered merge (ox=406, oy=230) → 2938x1652 → fit world 3344x1882 → WebP q92; see docs/design/background-tile-merge-guide.md theme 1
froststeel-camp.usage: alternate camp background (image-ground): Froststeel night snow warcamp (aurora, moon, ice-blue, glacier fortress, frozen pond). WORLD = native 3344x1882, world_scale=1. The large open snow courtyard (center, spanning upper-center to lower foreground) is the walkable ground. ground.ratio 0.3619 PASSES the ratio gate (>= 0.2815). Selectable via the detail-panel background switcher.

emberforge-camp.source_reference: merged from 4x 1672x941 tiles (emberforge-bg-tile-{a,b,c,d}) — feathered merge (ox=506, oy=230) → fit world 3344x1882 → WebP q92; see docs/design/background-tile-merge-guide.md theme 2
emberforge-camp.usage: alternate camp background (image-ground): Emberforge volcanic stronghold (eruption horizon, blood-red sun, lava channels, forge-citadel + anvil, magma cannons). WORLD = native 3344x1882, world_scale=1. The wide open obsidian plaza (center, between the back-ridge city, the right forge complex, and the foreground lava channel + bridges) is the walkable ground. ground.ratio 0.3301 PASSES the ratio gate (>= 0.2815). Selectable via the detail-panel background switcher.

mirebog-camp.source_reference: merged from 4x 1672x941 tiles (mirebog-bg-tile-{a,b,c,d}) — feathered merge (ox=585, oy=230) → 2759x1652 → fit world 3344x1882 → WebP q92; see docs/design/background-tile-merge-guide.md theme 3
mirebog-camp.usage: alternate camp background (image-ground): Mirebog swamp warcamp at dusk (foggy green-grey sky, hazy sun, half-sunken ziggurat-temple silhouette, right stilt-village on timber piles, boardwalks/jetties, coracles, croc pen, glowing mushrooms, swamp totems, will-o-wisp lanterns). WORLD = native 3344x1882, world_scale=1. The large open muddy clearing (center, ringed by the bog clutter and the foreground boardwalks) is the walkable ground. ground.ratio 0.3274 PASSES the ratio gate (>= 0.2815). Selectable via the detail-panel background switcher.

sunscorch-camp.source_reference: merged from 4x 1672x941 tiles (sunscorch-bg-tile-{a,b,c,d}) — feathered merge (ox=400, oy=230) → 2944x1652 → fit world 3344x1882 → WebP q92; see docs/design/background-tile-merge-guide.md theme 4
sunscorch-camp.usage: alternate camp background (image-ground): Sunscorch desert warcamp at bright midday (pale-blue sky, harsh sun, half-buried bone-colossus + ziggurat silhouettes, left war-bazaar of market tents, right water cistern + arena fighting-pit + beast-lines of pack lizards, dunes/cactus/bone piles/supply wagons). WORLD = native 3344x1882, world_scale=1. The large open sun-baked sand arena (center, skirting right of the left bazaar and clear of the right cistern/arena complex) is the walkable ground. ground.ratio 0.2927 PASSES the ratio gate (>= 0.2815). Selectable via the detail-panel background switcher.
